description: Per (path, category, subcategoryL1) SLA table consumed by the PGR escalation scheduler. `slaHoursByState` keys map 1:1 to the 6 PGR/CRS workflow states. A cell can be null (fall back to CRS.StateSLA), a single number (hours), or a [min,max] array. When ranged, the scheduler uses MAX for breach detection; the UI displays the range. uniqueIdentifier convention: `<path>:<category>:<subcategoryL1>`. Operators populate rows via the configurator UI or the CSV importer; nothing is auto-seeded.

path.description: Tenant-defined routing path (e.g. an institutional silo, a geographic region, or any tenant-meaningful grouping). The scheduler treats it as an opaque key.

slaHoursByState.description: Per-workflow-state SLA values. Each cell is normally a number (hours) but may also be a 2-element [min,max] tuple when the row models a range. NOTE: validation of the cell shape (number | [min,max] | null) is enforced application-side because the egov-mdms-v2 validator throws ClassCastException on JSON-Schema `oneOf` variants mixing number/array. Bounds (0 < n < 8760) likewise enforced client-side.

slaHoursByLevel.description: Optional per-escalation-level SLA hours; index = escalation level ([L0, L1, L2, ...]). Each cell is a number (hours) or null (fall through to the next SLA source). NOTE: items is deliberately {} because the egov-mdms-v2 validator throws ClassCastException on JSON-Schema oneOf unions mixing number/null; cell shape and bounds are enforced application-side (0 < n <= 8760; zero/negative cells are ignored and fall through), same precedent as slaHoursByState additionalProperties: true.

description: Singleton: per-tenant default SLA (in hours) per workflow state. Used by the escalation scheduler whenever the matching CRS.CategorySLA cell is null. Operators populate this via the configurator before relying on the CategorySLA layer; nothing is auto-seeded. uniqueIdentifier is always `default`.

singletonKey.description: Always 'default' — uniqueness key so the MDMS v2 validator can find a non-empty x-unique. Singleton record per tenant.

description: Append-only audit trail for changes to CRS.CategorySLA and CRS.StateSLA. One entry per save (per row for inline edits, one summary entry per bulk-import). uniqueIdentifier convention: `<timestamp>:<userUuid>:<recordIdentifier>` so concurrent writes don't collide on the same identifier. `beforeJson` / `afterJson` are serialized snapshots of the affected record.

Maps a tenant's workflow state names (e.g. PENDINGFORASSIGNMENT, PENDINGATLME for PGR; or BRD §5.2 names for CRS) onto the canonical SLA-column keys the scheduler uses (new, triage, forwarded, investigation, awaiting, resolved). Without this mapping, the scheduler cannot resolve which SLA cell applies to a complaint in a given state. Singleton-shaped: one record per tenant; uniqueIdentifier always "default".

mappings.description: Map of workflow state name → canonical SLA key (one of: new, triage, forwarded, investigation, awaiting, resolved). Operator-defined.

description: Tenant-wide escalation policy: max depth, per-level default SLAs (PRD complaint-type x level model), pre-breach warning config, manual-ESCALATE comment requirement. Singleton per tenant (uniqueIdentifier 'default'). All fields optional except singletonKey so tenants adopt incrementally; scheduler falls back to v0 RAINMAKER-PGR.EscalationConfig then static config when absent.
